titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Roger Jackson Collection of Henry Miller and Irving Stettner
Jackson (Roger) Collection of Henry Miller and Irving Stettner
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 19, 2025 at 23:05

did.unittitle: Roger Jackson collection of Henry Miller and Irving Stettner

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains printed material, writings, correspondence, and artwork by or relating to publisher Roger Jackson and authors Henry Miller and Irving Stettner.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Roger Jackson Papers Relating to Henry Miller, 1993–2012. II. Roger Jackson Collection of Irving Stettner, 1978–2003.

bioghist.head: Roger Jackson (1948–)
bioghist.p: Roger Jackson is an American collector, editor, and small press publisher of works by and about Henry Miller. Based in Ann Arbor, Michigan since 1993, he has published over 120 works including books, broadsides, and ephemera. He edited several bibliographies, catalogs, and other reference works about Miller, including Henry Miller: A Bibliography of Primary Sources (1993–1994), Henry Miller: A Personal Archive (1994), and Henry Miller: His Life in Ephemera, 1914–1980 (2012). Since 2004 Jackson has been managing editor of Nexus: The International Henry Miller Journal .

bioghist.head: Henry Miller (1891–1980)
bioghist.p: Henry Miller was an American author and painter. A full biographical note for Henry Miller is provided in the finding aid for the Henry Miller Papers (YCAL MSS 472).

bioghist.head: Irving Stettner (1922–2004)
bioghist.p: Irving Stettner was an American poet, author, and watercolorist who maintained friendships with Miller and Jackson. He was editor and publisher of Stroker , a small art and literary magazine to which Miller regularly contributed from 1978 until his death in 1980. His books Henry Miller, Anaïs Nin, and Me (1999) and On Henry Miller and the Wo Hop Mystery (1995) were published by Jackson, with whom Stettner left his correspondence with Miller for safekeeping.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Roger Jackson Collection of Henry Miller and Irving Stettner. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains printed material, writings, correspondence, and artwork by or relating to publisher Roger Jackson and authors Henry Miller and Irving Stettner. Printed material and ephemera produced by Roger Jackson are largely by and about Miller, and includes the works of Natasha Bernstein, Elmer Gertz, Peter Gowland, Bern Porter, Jack L. Saunders, Irving Stettner, Hoki Tokuda, Pascal Vrebos, and Colin Wilson. The collection also contains Jackson's proposed editing projects, original and photocopied correspondence of Miller and Stettner, and watercolor paintings by Stettner.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Seventy-seven volumes of Stroker received with the collection were removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Roger Jackson Collection of Henry Miller and Irving Stettner is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Roger Jackson Papers Relating to Henry Miller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains printed material published by Roger Jackson from 1993 to 2012; advertising fliers, circulars, and brochures for his publishing house; two editing projects by Jackson; and a catalog of Jackson's publications.

Printed material consists of books, pamphlets, cards, and broadsides, the majority of which are by or about Henry Miller. Reference works by Jackson include two volumes of Henry Miller: A Bibliography of Primary Sources ; Henry Miller: A Bibliography of Watercolors ; Henry Miller: A Personal Archive ; Henry Miller and the "Nudies," 1953–1997 ; and Henry Miller: His Life in Ephemera, 1914–1980 . Publications featuring the work of Henry Miller include Farewell from France: A Letter to Huntington Cairns, April 30 1939 ; From "Tropic of Cancer" ; and The Mezzotints . Other works about Miller include Henry Miller: Book of Tributes, 1931–1994 ; Henry Miller and Elmer Gertz: Selected Letters, 1964–1975 ; and The Henry Miller–Herbert Read Letters, 1935–1958 .

Works by Irving Stettner in this series consist of the books Henry Miller, Anaïs Nin, and Me and On Henry Miller and the Wo Hop Mystery , and two illustrated postcards. There are also over forty publications by artist and writer Bern Porter, including Bern Porter's Pillow Book ; My Affair with Anaïs Nin ; Questions about Henry Miller That No One Ever Asked Me–With Answers ; So Far: A Bern Porter Miscellany ; These 50 Years Gone ; and The World of Bern . Other writers present in this series include Natasha Bernstein, Elmer Gertz, Peter Gowland, Jack L. Saunders, Hoki Tokuda, Pascal Vrebos, and Colin Wilson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Printed Material, Publisher's Advertisements, Unpublished Works by Roger Jackson, and Catalog of Works Published by Roger Jackson. Within each subseries, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains approximately 190 editions of all 121 works published by Jackson from 1993 to 2012, including books, pamphlets, cards, and broadsides. Printed material is arranged in chronological order by publication date, and titles with multiple editions are grouped together following the earliest printing. All publications were assigned catalog numbers from 1 to 121 by Jackson. Unique editions of the same title are identified by a letter after the catalog number.

did.unittitle: #29b. Porter, Bern. "A Sex Oriented, Woman Connected Guy Doing His Own Thing": Bern Porter on Henry Miller, A Manuscript Sample
#104a. Gowland, Peter. Henry Miller: 18 Individual Portraits and an Introductory Essay
#107. Jackson, Roger. Henry Miller and the "Nudies," 1953–1997
#111. Porter, Bern. The World of Bern
#112b. Vrebos, Pascal. 444 Ocampo Drive: A Crazy Week with Henry Miller
#121. Jackson, Roger. Henry Miller: His Life in Ephemera, 1914–1980
#121. Supplementary materials to Henry Miller: His Life in Ephemera, 1914–1980
"Henry Miller and The New York Times , 1939–1999: A Research Bibliography"
Catalog of Works Published by Roger Jackson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This catalog, prepared for the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library by Jackson, contains detailed bibliographical information for all 121 works in the Printed Material subseries. Each entry lists a catalog number, title, author or editor, edition, page count, trim size, binding and paper type, illustration count, ISBN, publication date, and publication price, as well as images and publisher's notes pertaining to the work. The catalog also includes Jackson's description of other material in this collection.

did.unittitle: Roger Jackson Collection of Irving Stettner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains letters exchanged by Irving Stettner and Henry Miller from 1978 to 1980, and watercolor paintings by Stettner.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Henry Miller and Irving Stettner Correspondence, and Watercolors.

did.unittitle: Henry Miller and Irving Stettner Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of photocopies of Miller's letters to Stettner and Stettner's original letters to Miller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains fourteen watercolors, many of which Stettner inscribed to Jackson.
